title: carex canescens subsp. arctiformis
intro: Carex canescens subsp. arctiformis, also known as mountain sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carex canescens subsp. arctiformis include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations of mountain sedge can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
